ucs.title: [LLM] Black Basta (Storm-1811) email-bomb → Teams pretext → Quick Assist / RMM hands-on-keyboard payload stage
ucs.description: Hunts the signature 2024+ Black Basta initial-access chain documented by Microsoft Threat Intelligence (Storm-1811): adversary floods a target inbox with thousands of newsletter sign-ups (email bomb), then phones the user via external Teams chat impersonating IT support, talks them into launching Quick Assist or AnyDesk, and types in a PowerShell/cURL/cert-util one-liner that pulls the Black Basta loader. We pivot off the rare combination of (a) an inbound mail burst on the recipient, then (b) Quick Assist / AnyDesk / ScreenConnect spawning a payload-class child process within hours.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("quickassist.exe","anydesk.exe","teamviewer.exe","screenconnect.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","msiexec.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") NOT (Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename user as recipient | join type=inner recipient [ | tstats summariesonly=t count as inbound_burst from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.message_direction="inbound" by _time span=30m All_Email.recipient | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | where inbound_burst >= 30 | rename recipient as recipient ] | eval firstTime=strftime(firstTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), lastTime=strftime(lastTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S") | table firstTime lastTime dest recipient parent_process_name child cmdline inbound_burst
ucs.kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let BurstWindow = 30m;
let BurstThreshold = 30;          // 30+ inbound mails to one recipient in 30 min = email-bomb signal
let PostBombWindow = 6h;          // RMM payload typically runs within hours of the bomb / Teams call
let RmmTools = dynamic(["quickassist.exe","anydesk.exe","teamviewer.exe","screenconnect.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe"]);
let PayloadChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","msiexec.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let EmailBomb =
    EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
    | summarize InboundBurst = count(), DistinctSenders = dcount(SenderFromAddress)
               by RecipientEmailAddress, BurstBin = bin(Timestamp, BurstWindow)
    | where InboundBurst >= BurstThreshold and DistinctSenders >= 20   // many distinct senders = real bomb, not a thread
    | project RecipientEmailAddress, BurstBin, InboundBurst;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (RmmTools)
| where FileName in~ (PayloadChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-enc","DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","curl","certutil -urlcache","bitsadmin /transfer","-WindowStyle Hidden","FromBase64String")
     or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "quickassist.exe"   // any child of QuickAssist is unusual enough to keep
| join kind=inner EmailBomb on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where Timestamp between (BurstBin .. BurstBin + PostBombWindow)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn,
          RmmTool = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          RmmCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildProcess = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          BurstBin, InboundBurst,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, BurstBin)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let PostBombWindow = 6h;
let RmmTools = dynamic(["quickassist.exe","anydesk.exe","teamviewer.exe","screenconnect.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe"]);
let PayloadChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","msiexec.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
// 1. Email-bomb signal via OfficeActivity Exchange (delivered messages)
let EmailBomb =
    OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Receive","MailItemsAccessed","Deliver")
    | extend Recipient = tolower(tostring(MailboxOwnerUPN))
    | summarize InboundBurst = count(), DistinctSenders = dcount(tostring(Sender))
               by Recipient, BurstBin = bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
    | where InboundBurst >= 30 and DistinctSenders >= 20
    | project Recipient, BurstBin, InboundBurst;
// 2. RMM tool spawning a payload-class child
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName,"\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1])),
         Upn = tolower(strcat(SubjectUserName,"@",SubjectDomainName))
| where ParentLeaf in (RmmTools)
| where ChildLeaf in (PayloadChildren)
| where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-enc","DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","curl","certutil -urlcache","bitsadmin /transfer","-WindowStyle Hidden","FromBase64String")
     or ParentLeaf == "quickassist.exe"
| join kind=inner EmailBomb on $left.Upn == $right.Recipient
| where TimeGenerated between (BurstBin .. BurstBin + PostBombWindow)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, RmmTool = ParentLeaf,
          ChildProcess = ChildLeaf, CommandLine, BurstBin, InboundBurst,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, BurstBin)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Black Basta / Storm-1811 Quick Assist or RMM Spawning Payload Loader
id: 6f3a7b91-44a3-4b8e-9b02-bb3f6e2c0a1d
status: experimental
description: Detects Quick Assist (built-in MS remote-control tool abused by Storm-1811 → Black Basta) or third-party RMM software spawning script-host / LOLBin children with download or encoded-command tells. The user-typed payload after a vishing-driven Quick Assist takeover is the documented Black Basta initial-access pattern.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2024/05/15/threat-actors-misusing-quick-assist-in-social-engineering-attacks-leading-to-ransomware/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-131a
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/05/15
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\quickassist.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
      - '\TeamViewer.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.exe'
      - '\ConnectWiseControl.Client.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_intent:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - '-enc'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'certutil -urlcache'
      - 'bitsadmin /transfer'
      - '-WindowStyle Hidden'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_intent
fields:
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Genuine help-desk remote support sessions where the technician downloads diagnostics tooling. Verify by ticket.
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.t1566.004
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1204.002
ucs.rationale: Bare detections on Quick Assist or AnyDesk are useless — these tools are also used legitimately by IT. What is uniquely Black Basta / Storm-1811 is the *combination* of a recipient-targeted email bomb followed within hours by Quick Assist (or a stand-up AnyDesk install) spawning a script host that downloads code. CISA AA24-131A and Microsoft's May 2024 Storm-1811 advisory both document this exact

ucs.title: [LLM] Black Basta encryptor: bulk file rename to .basta extension by a single process
ucs.description: Catches Black Basta's terminal kill-chain step: the encryptor binary (often pushed via PsExec, GPO or BITS) walking the filesystem and rewriting victim files with the `.basta` (and historically `.bazta` / `.cipher`) extension while dropping `instructions_read_me.txt` ransom notes. Detection is keyed on a *single initiating process* causing a high count of rename/create events for these specific extensions inside a short window — the exact behavioural fingerprint of the Black Basta encryptor binary.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count as fileCount min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Filesystem.file_name) as sample_files dc(Filesystem.file_path) as distinct_paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.basta" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bazta" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.cipher" OR Filesystem.file_name="instructions_read_me.txt" OR Filesystem.file_name="readme.txt") Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_id span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where fileCount > 50 OR (file_name="*.basta" AND fileCount > 5) | eval firstSeen=strftime(firstSeen,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), lastSeen=strftime(lastSeen,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S") | sort - fileCount
ucs.kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let BastaExt = dynamic([".basta",".bazta",".cipher"]);
let NoteNames = dynamic(["instructions_read_me.txt","readme.txt"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where ActionType in ("FileRenamed","FileCreated","FileModified")
| where (FileName endswith ".basta")
     or (FileName endswith ".bazta")
     or (FileName endswith ".cipher")
     or (FileName in~ (NoteNames) and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| extend IsBastaExt = (FileName endswith ".basta" or FileName endswith ".bazta" or FileName endswith ".cipher"),
         IsRansomNote = (FileName in~ (NoteNames))
| summarize
    BastaRenames    = countif(IsBastaExt),
    RansomNoteDrops = countif(IsRansomNote),
    DistinctFolders = dcount(FolderPath),
    FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
    SampleFiles = make_set_if(strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName), IsBastaExt, 10),
    SampleNotes = make_set_if(strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName), IsRansomNote, 5)
    by DeviceName, DeviceId,
       InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
       InitiatingProcessAccountName,
       Window = bin(Timestamp, 10m)
| where (BastaRenames >= 5)            // even a small burst of .basta renames is high-fidelity
     or (BastaRenames >= 1 and RansomNoteDrops >= 3 and DistinctFolders >= 3)
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let NoteNames = dynamic(["instructions_read_me.txt","readme.txt"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| extend FileNameLower = tolower(TargetFileName),
         PathLower = tolower(TargetFilePath)
| where FileNameLower endswith ".basta"
     or FileNameLower endswith ".bazta"
     or FileNameLower endswith ".cipher"
     or (FileNameLower in (NoteNames) and not(PathLower startswith "c:\\windows\\") and not(PathLower startswith "c:\\program files"))
| extend IsBastaExt = (FileNameLower endswith ".basta" or FileNameLower endswith ".bazta" or FileNameLower endswith ".cipher"),
         IsRansomNote = (FileNameLower in (NoteNames))
| summarize
    BastaRenames = countif(IsBastaExt),
    RansomNoteDrops = countif(IsRansomNote),
    DistinctFolders = dcount(TargetFilePath),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
    SampleFiles = make_set_if(TargetFilePath, IsBastaExt, 10)
    by DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, ActorUsername, Window = bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where (BastaRenames >= 5)
     or (BastaRenames >= 1 and RansomNoteDrops >= 3 and DistinctFolders >= 3)
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Black Basta Ransomware File Extension Drop
id: 8a2c4d77-1b6e-4c0a-8c44-2e1a9b7c6d11
status: experimental
description: Detects creation/rename of files with the `.basta`, `.bazta` or `.cipher` extensions used by the Black Basta ransomware family. A single hit is high-confidence evidence of active encryption.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-131a
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/black-basta-summary-2022
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/05/15
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.basta'
      - '.bazta'
      - '.cipher'
  condition: selection
fields:
  - Image
  - User
  - TargetFilename
falsepositives:
  - Researchers studying Black Basta samples in a sandbox.
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
ucs.rationale: The `.basta` and `.bazta` file extensions, plus the dual `readme.txt` / `instructions_read_me.txt` note pattern, are exclusive to the Black Basta family (Conti-spinoff, Russian-speaking criminal crew, active since April 2022 — referenced in CISA AA24-131A and Mandiant/CrowdStrike reporting). The detection is triggered by the encryptor itself, so it survives every pre-encryption obfuscation step (r
